old_claim.note: Book Binding (Codex Production) provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Illuminated manuscripts are decorated handwritten books, so codex and book-production craft supplies the bound-page format for the scoped artifacts.

source_shape.source_locator: Article defines the codex as a folded pre-print manuscript book form and discusses its displacement of scrolls, supporting book/codex production as the artifact format for illuminated manuscripts.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source establishes the codex as the manuscript book form used before print culture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because this node is scoped to decorated handwritten books rather than isolated painting, codex production supplies the physical book architecture for the illuminated artifact.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: illuminated_manuscripts_scribal_art keeps book_binding_codex_production as an enabling format edge.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed to loose painted leaves rather than bound manuscript books.
A stronger source shows the scoped early examples were not codices.
A separate parchment_codex_illumination node is added and should replace this broader book-binding prerequisite.
short_reason: Illuminated books require manuscript-book format context.
